description: Operations dashboard for the claude-alert-analyzer services (k8s-analyzer + checkmk-analyzer). Covers the pipeline counters, Claude API latency/errors, token-cost analytics, agentic tool-loop, and storm-robustness signals exposed at /metrics.

panels.description: Webhook requests received per minute on the main HTTP port.
panels.title: Webhooks received

panels.description: Alerts that completed analysis and were published to ntfy.
panels.title: Alerts processed

panels.description: Alerts where analysis or publishing failed. Sustained non-zero indicates Claude-API or ntfy issues.
panels.title: Alerts failed

panels.description: Alerts dropped because the work queue was full. Should be 0 in steady state — sustained non-zero means workers can't keep up.
panels.title: Queue-full drops

panels.description: Successfully analyzed alerts per source (k8s vs. checkmk).
panels.title: Alert rate by source

panels.description: Alert volume split by normalized severity bucket (critical / warning / info).
panels.title: Alert rate by severity

panels.description: Alerts skipped because a duplicate fingerprint or group key is in cooldown.
panels.title: Cooldown drops

panels.description: Alerts currently waiting in the work queue per source. Climbs when workers can't keep up; persistent rise precedes queue-full drops.
panels.title: Queue depth

panels.description: Claude API call latency percentiles across all sources.
panels.title: Claude API latency

panels.description: Claude API errors per second by source. Drives the circuit-breaker if enabled.
panels.title: Claude API errors

panels.description: Mean per-alert end-to-end processing time (webhook -> ntfy publish), derived from the unlabeled summary.
panels.title: Per-alert processing duration (mean)

panels.description: ntfy publish failures by source. Sustained non-zero means the analyses produce results that aren't reaching operators.
panels.title: ntfy publish errors

panels.description: Cache-hit ratio = read / (read + creation + input). Below 30% indicates caching is not paying off; above 60% means caching is working well. See docs/cost-and-storm-protection.md.

targets.expr: (sum(rate(alert_analyzer_claude_tokens_total{kind="cache_read", product=~"$product"}[5m])) / clamp_min(sum(rate(alert_analyzer_claude_tokens_total{kind="cache_read", product=~"$product"}[5m]) + rate(alert_analyzer_claude_tokens_total{kind="cache_creation", product=~"$product"}[5m]) + rate(alert_analyzer_claude_tokens_total{kind="input", product=~"$product"}[5m])), 1e-9)) or vector(0)

panels.title: Cache hit rate

panels.description: Token rate by category (input / output / cache_creation / cache_read). Use to spot loops burning tokens or sudden spikes.
panels.title: Token rate by category

panels.description: Input + cache-read tokens per model. Use to compare cost between configured Claude models (e.g. opus vs. haiku per severity).
panels.title: Input tokens by model

panels.description: Output tokens per model. Combined with input rate, ratio output/input > 0.15 may indicate runaway agentic loops.
panels.title: Output tokens by model

panels.description: Tool calls inside the agentic Claude loop, broken down by tool and outcome. Watch for rejected_validation / rejected_verb spikes.
panels.title: Tool calls by tool / outcome

panels.description: Per-tool wall-clock latency p95. Hard ceiling is the per-call timeout enforced by the handlers.

targets.expr: (histogram_quantile(0.95, sum by (le, tool) (rate(alert_analyzer_agent_tool_duration_seconds_bucket{product=~"$product"}[5m]))) and on(tool) (sum by (tool) (rate(alert_analyzer_agent_tool_duration_seconds_count{product=~"$product"}[5m])) > 0)) or vector(0)

panels.title: Tool latency (p95)

panels.description: Distribution of tool-rounds Claude actually used per loop. Compare with the 'rounds exhausted' panel to see how often the loop ended naturally vs. hit the cap.

targets.expr: (histogram_quantile(0.50, sum by (le, product) (rate(alert_analyzer_agent_rounds_per_run_bucket{product=~"$product"}[5m]))) and (sum(rate(alert_analyzer_agent_rounds_per_run_count{product=~"$product"}[5m])) > 0)) or vector(0)

(histogram_quantile(0.95, sum by (le, product) (rate(alert_analyzer_agent_rounds_per_run_bucket{product=~"$product"}[5m]))) and (sum(rate(alert_analyzer_agent_rounds_per_run_count{product=~"$product"}[5m])) > 0)) or vector(0)

panels.title: Rounds used per loop

panels.description: Loops that ended via forced summary because MAX_AGENT_ROUNDS was reached. Sustained non-zero may mean the budget is too tight or Claude is in a redundant loop.
panels.title: Rounds-exhausted rate

panels.description: Storm-mode gauge per source. 1 = the 5-minute alert window exceeds STORM_MODE_THRESHOLD; all severities are forced to rounds=0 while active. Page on dwell-time > 5 min.
panels.title: Storm-mode active

panels.description: Circuit-breaker state per source. 0 = closed, 1 = open, 2 = half-open. Page on dwell-time > 2 min in state 1.
panels.title: Claude API circuit-breaker state

panels.description: Aggregator drops by aggregator type (storm | breaker). Sustained non-zero means the aggregation interval is too long for the current alert volume, or the publisher is failing.
panels.title: Notify-aggregator drops

panels.description: /webhook HTTP outcomes per second by outcome label. accepted = 2xx; auth_failed = 401; payload_invalid = 400; payload_too_large = 413; unavailable = 503 (queue full); internal_error = 5xx.
panels.title: Webhook outcomes

panels.description: Active goroutines per binary. Sustained growth indicates a goroutine leak.
panels.title: Goroutines

title: Claude Alert Analyzer
